subject: Physics
title: The Third Law: Approaching Zero
description: A fast breakdown of The Third Law: Approaching Zero for builders.

hook.text: The third law says you can get as close to absolute zero as you want, but you can never actually reach it.

buildup.text: As temperature drops toward zero Kelvin, removing each additional bit of heat requires more and more energy.

discovery.text: At absolute zero, a perfect crystal would have zero entropy—complete order with no randomness.

twist.text: In practice, reaching exactly zero would require an infinite number of cooling steps. It's mathematically impossible.

climax.text: Scientists have reached nanokelvin temperatures—billionths of a degree above zero—but zero itself remains out of reach.

punchline.text: Infinite steps to reach zero. Close but never there.

quiz.question: Why is absolute zero unattainable?
options: Each step closer requires exponentially more energy, needing infinite steps
No thermometer can measure that low
